title: Sports Analytics in Practice
text:
Welcome!
Welcome to the course website dedicated to the ULMS744 module: Sports Analytics in Practice! On this site, you will find the R (coding) seminar materials. You can navigate to each week’s material by clicking on the specific sidebar menu link. Please do let me know if you are struggling to access anything. Below you will find information related to the organisational aspects of the course. These are also covered in the intro lecture slides.

title: Sports Analytics in Practice
text:
1 Key Details
Shunya Kimura will be taking Semester 2 Weeks 07 – 11.
Contact: s.kimura@liverpool.ac.uk (book a meeting) (link to Bookings with me)
The module is also supported by Ayan Orujob who will be taking over the first six weeks. He offers his own office hours for additional support outside of the lectures/seminars, and he’ll let you know when they take place.
Keep an eye on the module announcements page for further updates and consult Canvas for all essential module information, including lecture slides/recordings, reading lists and key details about assignments submission etc. This workbook is designed primarily to support the seminar sessions, and it contains the key materials, exercises and guidance that we will work through during seminars.

title: Sports Analytics in Practice
text:
Help & Support
For specific assistance with this module, you can:

Refer to the Canvas assignments tab for queries about module assignments
Ask a question at the end of lectures or during the seminars
Book into the Office Hours (link to Bookings with me)

title: Sports Analytics in Practice
text:
Acknowledgements
This workbook is created using the Quarto publishing system. It takes inspiration and design elements from:

The GEOG0030: Geocomputation workbook by Justin van Dijk
The POLS0010: Data Analysis 2024 workbook by James Cheshire
The GEOG0051: Mining Social and Geographic Datasets 2020 materials by Stephen Law
The POLS0010: Data Analysis 2019 materials by Tom O’Grady
